title: New bills, old battles, changed equations: What awaits Parliament this Monsoon Session
content: Parliament's Monsoon Session begins Monday with a packed legislative agenda. The government aims to pass the Women's Reservation Bill after its earlier defeat. Opposition parties face electoral losses and alliance collapses, altering parliamentary arithmetic. Several key bills are listed, including the FCRA and Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhishthan. The government hopes to secure passage with adjusted strategies and potential support.

title: Messi creates history, becomes first captain to lead a nation in three WC finals
content: Lionel Messi captained Argentina in his third World Cup final. He previously led his team in the 2014 and 2022 finals. Messi aims to win consecutive World Cup titles for Argentina. Argentina reached the final after a comeback win against England. Spain advanced after defeating France in their semifinal match.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: Yamal creates history, becomes first teenager to play in both Euro & World Cup finals
content: Lamine Yamal achieved a historic football milestone before the World Cup final. He became the first teenager to play in both a Euro and a World Cup final. Yamal was instrumental in Spain's journey to the championship match. The young player's performance has been a key factor for his team. Spain faced Argentina in the title clash for the coveted trophy.

title: ARG vs ESP WC final: How much the champions will earn from a record $871M prize fund
content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will award a record $51 million to the champions. Runners-up will receive $34 million, and the total prize pool reaches $871 million. Every participating nation is guaranteed at least $12.5 million for qualifying and preparation. This expanded prize money reflects FIFA's increased financial commitment to the tournament. Prize money goes to national federations, not directly to the players.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: Elisha Cuthbert sells LA home for $1.93M after $1M price cut
content: Hollywood actress Elisha Cuthbert has sold her Los Angeles home for $1.93 million after several unsuccessful attempts to find a buyer. The property was sold shortly after a final price cut. The sale comes as Cuthbert returns to acting following a four-year break, during which she focused on raising her two children with husband Dion Phaneuf.

title: Warming seas bring octopus and Mediterranean fish into UK waters
content: As the seas warm, British waters are now seeing an influx of Mediterranean species. This environmental transformation is compelling local fishers to modify their traditional techniques. Notably, octopus populations are surging, impacting native shellfish and depleting other seafood supplies. In response, chefs are creatively revamping menus to highlight these new arrivals like octopus and bream, balancing the challenges with exciting culinary possibilities.

title: Can your builder change project plans after selling your flat? Here's what RERA says
content: RERA protects homebuyers from unauthorized builder changes to sanctioned plans.  Promoters need buyer consent for alterations beyond minor structural necessities.  Changes to common areas require consent from two-thirds of allottees.  Builders remain liable for structural defects for five years after possession.  Homebuyers can seek relief from the Authority for unauthorized changes.

title: Streamer University 2026 ending date explained as Kai Cenat reveals what happens before July 20
content: The grand finale of Streamer University 2026, hosted by Kai Cenat, is set for July 20th morning. Alumni have begun to flock to campus to engage with students in these last days of festivities. Kai has made it clear that any rule infractions could lead to expulsion from campus. This year's event boasts a longer duration than its predecessor, designed to maximize creative collaboration and ensure a vibrant atmosphere.

title: How much prize money does the FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place winner get? Full payout explained
content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will award twenty-nine million dollars for third place. The fourth-place team will receive twenty-seven million dollars after the playoff. This prize money is separate from preparation payments given to nations. The champions will earn fifty million dollars and runners-up thirty-three million dollars. FIFA confirmed prize money distribution for all tournament stages.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce frustrated over wedding controversy: Report
content: The Kansas City Chiefs popular tight end Travis Kelce is all set to bounce  back on the field for his 15th season in the league, weeks after marrying Taylor Swift, as millions cheer him on. While both Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift have remained strictly private about their lives, it seems the two are now anticipating a few issues in their lives.

title: Connor Bedard’s agent shares about his contract negotiations with Chicago Blackhawks
content: Connor Bedard signed a five-year, $75 million contract extension with the Chicago Blackhawks. This deal carries an average annual value of $15 million for the young star. Bedard intentionally chose a lower salary to provide team cap flexibility. His agent confirmed Bedard prioritized strengthening the roster over maximizing personal earnings. Bedard expressed happiness and belief in the Blackhawks organization and its future.

title: Rohit Sharma scripts history, becomes first Indian to score ODI ton at Lord's
content: Rohit Sharma achieved a historic ODI century at Lord's on Sunday. He became the oldest Indian to score an ODI hundred in the format. This remarkable innings came amidst scrutiny regarding his ODI future. England posted a massive 387 runs, setting a daunting target for India. Sharma's century marked his eighth ODI hundred in England.

title: Rohit Sharma rewrites ODI history, sets record for most hundreds in a visiting country
content: Rohit Sharma achieved a significant milestone by scoring his eighth ODI century in England. This remarkable century placed him ahead of Sachin Tendulkar on an elite all-time list. England posted a formidable 387/3, setting a challenging target for India. Sharma's innings helped India remain competitive in the series-deciding match. The Indian opener's performance silenced critics after a lean run.

title: 'Wangchuk to end fast if ... ': Sonam's wife Gitanjali on Parliament march
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk may end his hunger strike if leaders assure parliamentary action. His wife alleges illegal detention and denied choice of hospital for treatment. She claims a trust deficit in the government's handling of his health. Wangchuk was moved from Jantar Mantar amid concerns about his participation in a march. Discrepancies in his medical reports and police presence have raised further questions.

title: Al Pacino’s quote on judgment: A timeless lesson on looking beyond first impressions
content: Al Pacino, turning eighty-six in April 2026, continues his prolific acting career. He recently premiered 'Killing Castro' and received the Sam Wanamaker Award for his theatre contributions. A quote from his 1996 film 'City Hall' emphasizes judging people with care and understanding. Pacino's life, like his quote suggests, is defined by the unseen mortar, not just visible bricks.

title: First time ever at Lord's! India, England script unique ODI record with century stands
content: India and England created ODI history at Lord's by becoming the first teams to both register 100-plus opening partnerships in the same match at the venue. Ben Duckett and Jacob Bethell added 192 for England before Rohit Sharma and Shubman Gill replied with 147. Gill's dismissal for 77 ended the record stand during India's chase of 388.

title: 'Intend to work with PM Modi': Ex-TMC leader Kakoli announces NDA entry with 20 MPs
content: Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar announced her group's move to join the ruling NDA in Parliament.  Twenty former TMC MPs are now aligning with the NCPI parliamentary group.  This group intends to work with Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Amit Shah.  They will support the government's initiatives and focus on constructive debate.  The rebels' realignment follows the TMC's recent election defeat in West Bengal.

title: ‘Security hole’ enabled US-Israel bombing of Ayatollah’s residence: Iran minister Araghchi
content: Iranian foreign minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi alleged a security breach allowed foreign powers access.  This breach reportedly provided intelligence and influenced decision-making during a conflict.  He stated attackers knew Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's routine and targeted his residence precisely.  A regional leader had warned Iran about leadership being targeted before the conflict began.  Araghchi noted the foreign ministry remained operational throughout the intense 40-day war.

title: Sleepless nights, depressing times, & then a chess Grandmaster: Making of Rathanvel VS
content: After four-and-a-half years of heartbreak, sleepless nights and repeated near-misses, Rathanvel VS finally crossed the 2500 Elo mark to become India's 99th Grandmaster. From discovering chess through a free board to overcoming injuries, financial setbacks and crushing disappointments, the 2001-born Coimbatore player's perseverance, family support and belief transformed a long-awaited dream into reality.

title: 'Forced to work 15hrs a day, tortured, no salary for months': Hyderabad woman stranded in Oman
content: A Hyderabad woman alleges forced labor and wage denial in Oman. She traveled for a housemaid job but faced poor conditions and no pay. The woman escaped and sought help from the Indian Embassy in Muscat. The embassy has acknowledged the matter and is investigating the situation. Officials are working to ensure her safety and arrange her return home.

title: Tanishk Bagchi deletes 'Saiyaara' Rs 8 lakh unpaid royalty post hours after YRF responds
content: Music composer Tanishk Bagchi claimed unpaid royalties for the Saiyaara title track. Yash Raj Films clarified all contractual payments were made to composers. Bagchi's social media post alleging financial disappointment was later deleted. The Saiyaara title track was a collaboration between three composers, YRF stated. Royalties are being distributed equally among all three composers as agreed.

title: 'No permission sought or granted': Delhi Police on CJP's Parliament march
content: Delhi Police has denied permission for a proposed march to Parliament on July 20. Prohibitory orders are in effect across the New Delhi district, banning assemblies. Unauthorized gatherings will face legal action under new Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita provisions. The Delhi High Court previously upheld the Centre's intervention in Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Authorities urge public cooperation to ensure security during the upcoming Parliament session.

title: 'Sorry papa': Kanpur BTech gold medallist dies by suicide after 50 failed job attempts
content: Tragedy struck in Kanpur as a 23-year-old BTech gold medallist ended his life, having faced the daunting challenge of applying for a government position more than fifty times without success. His poignant suicide note expressed sorrow towards his father for not meeting expectations. The police have launched an investigation and are exploring the likelihood that depression contributed to this sorrowful decision.

title: WC final preview: Why Spain vs Argentina is much more than Messi vs Yamal
content: Spain and Argentina meet in a World Cup final loaded with footballing destiny, La Masia symbolism, Messi’s last miracle, Yamal’s first coronation and enough off-field politics to make FIFA’s “football unites the world” slogan file for leave. Read more in today's OFFSIDE.

title: Gurnoor Brar enters unwanted record books with nightmare spell at Lord's
content: Gurnoor Brar recorded the most expensive ODI bowling figures at Lord's. His spell of 0 for 97 entered India's record books for expensive spells. England's top order dominated an inexperienced Indian bowling attack. Ben Duckett and Jacob Bethell built a strong opening partnership. England's total of 387 for 3 became the highest ODI score at Lord's.

title: 'Months of torture': Bengaluru woman dies by suicide after 'dowry harassment'
content: A 31-year-old woman allegedly died by suicide after enduring dowry harassment. Her husband and father-in-law are accused of causing her distress. The deceased's father filed a complaint with the police regarding the harassment. Police arrested the husband and father-in-law following the complaint. Both accused individuals have been remanded to judicial custody.

title: Joe Root scripts history, becomes first England batter to...
content: Joe Root achieved a historic milestone with six consecutive fifty-plus scores.  He scored an unbeaten 74 off 49 balls at Lord's.  England posted a strong 387 for 3 after batting first.  Ben Duckett and Jacob Bethell provided a solid opening partnership.  Jos Buttler finished with a quick 41 not out.

title: 'He intends to continue the fast': Sonam Wangchuk's wife vows to lead Parliament march
content: Gitanjali J Angmo will lead a march to Parliament representing her husband, Sonam Wangchuk. Wangchuk continues his indefinite hunger strike from Safdarjung Hospital after being moved there. The Delhi High Court declined to order his immediate transfer to a private facility. Angmo urged protesters to maintain peace and remain vigilant against disruptions. Security has been heightened across New Delhi ahead of the planned march.

title: Former NHL star Mike Commodore warns Oilers parents at development camp
content: Ex-NHL defenseman Mike Commodore has escalated his years-long war on the Edmonton Oilers by issuing a warning to parents attending the club’s development camp. The social media post went viral across the NHL, reigniting discussion of Commodore’s past with former coach Mike Babcock, and drawing mixed reactions from hockey fans and analysts.

title: 'Carry Tiranga, no political flags': CJP releases do's & don'ts for 'Chalo Sansad' march
content: The Cockroach Janta Party released guidelines for the 'Chalo Sansad' march. Participants are urged to maintain peace and carry national symbols. Activist Sonam Wangchuk called the march India's second freedom movement. Delhi Police have deployed additional personnel and intensified security measures. Authorities are determined to prevent any unauthorized march towards Parliament.

title: Ashwin questions Kuldeep's omission, says repeated snubs 'can affect confidence'
content: Ravichandran Ashwin questioned India's decision to omit Kuldeep Yadav.  The spinner was again left out of the side for the third ODI.  This repeated exclusion could negatively impact his confidence and rhythm.  India opted for a pace-heavy attack despite bowling absences.  Kuldeep has now missed all ODIs and T20Is on the tour.

title: Maharashtra allows restaurants, hotels to stay open till 4am for FIFA World Cup final
content: Maharashtra eateries can now stay open until 4 am on July 20. This allows football fans to watch the Argentina-Spain World Cup final publicly.  Hospitality bodies had requested this extension for licensed establishments.  Other cities like Delhi and Bengaluru have also announced similar relaxations.  The final match will begin early Sunday morning, Indian time.

title: 'Stay here for the night': Dipke calls for night-long vigil ahead of 'Chalo Sansad' march

title: 'Should've scored 707, got 217': NTA finds OMR tampering after NEET aspirants flag score gaps
content: the controversy began when a student from Pune alleged he had received two different OMR sheets . the national testing agency concluded that the sheets in question were manipulated . students raised allegations of discrepancies in their entrance exam results .

title: Chinese President Xi Jinping's strong message to American technology companies on AI
content: The TOI Tech Desk is a dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant news from the world of technology to readers of The Times of India. title: Geo-tagging of State’s forest locations opens the doors for trespassers
content: Forest department flags 23 Google Maps listings inside reserve forests in Kozhikode as misleading; officials say unauthorised map pins and uploaded photographs are encouraging illegal entry into protected areas; DFO seeks removal of the listings, warning of trespassing, public safety risks and human-wildlife conflict

title: Bihar CM opens ₹130.88-cr. link to divert floodwaters, enhance farm productivity
content: The link, part of a river interlinking project, will reduce intensity of floods in northern Bihar, says Choudhary; he also inaugurates 551 schools, lists measures to improve education infrastructure

title: At least eight dead, 15 hurt in Nagaland landslide
content: Heavy incessant rainfall and a cloudburst caused widespread destruction at various locations in and around Mon town; State announces ex-gratia of ₹4 lakh for kin of deceased, ₹74,000 for each of the injured

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Wangchuk will end fast if Parliament session focuses on education accountability, says Gitanjali Angmo
content: The Delhi Police say ‘no permission sought or granted’ for July 20 Parliament protest march and Section 163 is currently in effect in the New Delhi district

title: Students must unite against drugs: Minister Bindu Krishna
content: She was presiding over the distribution ceremony of the ‘Focus Kollam’ Minister’s Excellence Award-2026, organised for talented students from the Kollam constituency

title: Villagers oppose new stone quarry, crusher unit near Cheyyar in Tiruvannamalai
content: The quarry might pollute groundwater and waterbodies, and affect agricultural activities, say residents

title: Panel to consider two complaints on leasing and illegal diversion of forest land in Kerala
content: The complaints on reported illegal diversion of forest land and irregular timber auction in Chalakudy forest division and the alleged continued use of reserved forest land by Plantation Corporation of Kerala will come up before the Central Empowered Committee, New Delhi, on July 20

title: PMK organises signature campaign urging State govt. to notify extent of Pallikaranai marshland
content: Speaking during the campaign, the PMK president criticised former DMK minister P.K. Sekarbabu, who was in-charge of the CMDA, for not playing the crucial role of being the watchdog in the State for real estate development

title: Neha, a prominent face of the CJP protests, presses on with hunger strike
content: The All India Students’ Association’s national president and two of her colleagues continue the hunger strike demanding reforms in education. She vows to continue the fight to remind people that ‘the divisive politics that the BJP has taught’ is not their legacy

title: Move to resume Korapuzha dredging sparks call for scientific assessment
content: The environmental organisation calls for the constitution of an expert committee, including a marine ecologist and a representative of residents, to evaluate the proposal to deepen the estuary before any further decision is taken

title: Thousands gather at Jantar Mantar ahead of CJP’s march to Parliament
content: The Cockroach Janta Party’s designated protest site overflowed with unprecedented crowds following activist Sonam Wangchuk’s forced hospitalisation

title: West Bengal BJP government is implementing the promise of ‘Sonar Bangla’, says Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister virtually lays the foundation stone of the ₹700-crore Amul dairy project at Sankrail in Howrah

title: Will end fast if education accountability issue raised in Parliament Monsoon Session: Wife Gitanjali conveys Wangchuk’s message
content: “Sonam has requested all of us to take out tomorrow's march peacefully and ensure that the protest is not misused,” she said, conveying his message at the Jantar Mantar protest

title: INL links ‘move’ to reconstitute Waqf Board to Munambam
content: INL leader alleges the Chief Minister conspired with the Waqf Minister and the IUML to reconstitute the Waqf Board after the existing refused to compromise on the Munambam issue

title: Jammu and Kashmir rains updates: At least 11 dead, several missing in flash floods; rescue ops. under way
content: Home Minister Amit Shah assured the L-G and the CM of all possible assistance from the Centre for the safety and security of the affected people, officials said

title: DMK, TDP should uphold their legacy of ensuring fairness delimitation, says Congress
content: Congress leader says extending the 1971 Census freeze on Lok Sabha seat distribution is essential to preserve the federal balance

title: At least 11 killed, 10 missing as heavy rain triggers flash floods in J&K
content: Initial assessment suggested that more than 100 houses and around two dozen school structures were damaged in the floods

title: CIC directs IITs to form high-level committees to curb student deaths by suicides
content: It says information on panels must be proactively disclosed on the institutes’ official websites. The direction came while disposing of appeals filed by an IIT-Kanpur alumnus, who is the founder of a non-governmental organisation engaged in student rehabilitation and mental health counselling

title: Drought report to Centre in 15 days after comprehensive survey of ground realities, says DKS
content: ‘We have asked for a comprehensive field survey in all 178 taluks, of which 158 taluks have received very average rainfall, and 20 taluks have not received rainfall at all’

title: Martyrs’ Day 2026 turns out to be show of political strength for rival Trinamool factions in West Bengal
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, who, along with 19 other party Lok Sabha MPs, has merged with the little-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI), said the faction would observe Martyrs’ Day at Rajghat

title: All-party meet: MPs seek debate on NEET leak delimitation, temple theft, E20
content: Parties stage a brief walkout from all-party meet in protest against government’s invitation to breakaway Trinamool faction; leaders from 50 political parties who attended the meeting seek discussion on police action at Jantar Mantar and Manipur situation also

title: CBI conducts searches across four States in ₹1,109-crore bank fraud case
content: The case pertains to alleged cheating by the accused company, Gupta Power Infrastructure Ltd. According to the agency, it had taken various fund-based and non-fund based financial facilities from Canara Bank

title: Roads turn death traps for gig workers in Kerala
content: Over 30 such workers, mostly food delivery personnel, have been killed in road accidents in the State in the past one year, as per informal data collected by a union. Time constraints coupled with heavy traffic often contribute to the higher number of fatalities, those involved in the sector say

title: CJP march call: Section 163 imposed; security tightened in Delhi ahead of Monsoon Session
content: The heightened vigil comes as the CJP announced the march in protest against alleged NEET irregularities and to demand the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: Sleeping Couple Falls From 4-Storey Roof in Delhi, Man Dies, Wife Critical
content: Preliminary investigation revealed that the couple had dinner with the family around 9 pm and then went to sleep on the rooftop of the house.

title: Sirens, Strikes, Shattered Deal: Middle East On Edge As US, Iran Trade Blows
content: Iran's health ministry said at least 50 people had been killed and more than 500 wounded in the latest wave of US strikes.

title: Man Dies After Fan Falls On Him In Hospital, It Blames 'Medical Condition'
content: According to eyewitnesses, the ceiling fan suddenly came loose and fell directly onto the patient's bed, striking Akbar in the abdomen. He was immediately attended to by hospital staff.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: India Issues Travel Advisory As US Strikes Iran For 8th Straight Day
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: CENTCOM said the raids were designed to "swiftly punish Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces who launched attacks against American service members in Jordan last night."

title: 5.5 Magnitude Earthquake Kills At Least 5 In Peru's Andes Region
content: The US Geological Survey reported the quake struck on Saturday at 9:24 pm, with its epicentre located 2 kilometres (1.24 miles) west-southwest of the city of Sicaya in Huancayo province. It was 10 kilometres (6.21 miles) deep.

title: Israel Vows "Full Force" Response If Iran Fires Missiles
content: "If Iran fires missiles at Israel, we will attack them with full force," Israel Katz said during a visit to a rescue service facility.

title: After 2 Fatal Shootings, US Immigration Officers Wear Body Cameras On Stops
content: Homan's comments come after federal immigration agents fatally shot two men six days apart during traffic stops in Texas and Maine earlier this month. There is no body-camera footage from ICE agents involved in those incidents.

title: Delhi's New Rooftop Solar Campaign To Offer Subsidy, Help With Installation
content: The "Solarize East" campaign will initially cover South-East Delhi, with residents being guided on government subsidies, net metering and rooftop solar installation.

title: 66 Missing After 86-Year-Old Ferry Overturns In Guyana
content: The ferry MV Barima left the small English-speaking country at the northeast corner of South America Saturday afternoon with 133 people aboard for a trip from the capital, Georgetown, to the town of Port Kaituma.

title: 675 Parks, 4 Ridge Zones, 6.62 Lakh Plants: Delhi's Monsoon Plantation Push
content: DDA says it has completed nearly 30 per cent of its 23-lakh plantation target. The drive comes amid a wider push to track tree protection and compensatory plantation across Delhi.

title: Large Gatherings Banned, Cops Geared Up: Delhi's Plan For Parliament March
content: The Delhi Police have banned large gatherings in New Delhi district amid CJP's protest plan on a day the monsoon session of parliament will begin

title: Couple, Son, Killed After Car Rams Their Motorcycle In Madhya Pradesh
content: The car has been impounded and its driver taken into custody, the officer said, adding a case has been registered and further probe is underway.

title: Karnataka Congress Restructuring To Begin Tomorrow: State Party Chief
content: Hariprasad also dismissed speculation over his possible induction into the Karnataka Cabinet, while asserting that Cabinet formation is the sole prerogative of the chief minister.

title: FIFA's Biggest-Ever World Cup Is Also Its Most Polluting: Studies
content: Long before the tournament began, FIFA had made big promises of its own. Under its official targets, FIFA pledged to halve greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 and reach net-zero by 2040.

title: Domestic Worker Found Dead In Chandigarh Employer's Home, Suicide Suspected
content: Her family, who hails from Uttar Pradesh, told reporters that she started working at the house a week ago and was staying in one of the rooms there.

title: Pakistan Summons Imran Khan's Sister Over Remarks On Army, War With India
content: The National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency issued the summons on Saturday, asking Niazi to appear at its Islamabad office on Monday to record her statement.

title: 'Political Change In Bengal Unlike Any Since Independence': Amit Shah
content: Shah virtually laid the foundation stone of the world's largest curd production plant coming up in Howrah from the Biswa Bangla Convention Centre in eastern Kolkata's New Town area.

title: "No Permission": Prohibitory Orders Imposed Ahead Of Planned Parliament March
content: The prohibitory order under Section 163 BNSS (formerly Section 144 CrPC) is currently in effect in the New Delhi district, according to the DCP's post.

title: Police enhance night patrol, vehicle checks across Sri Sathya Sai district
content: During the inspections, the police officials question suspicious individuals moving during midnight hours without valid reasons, collect details and fingerprints of some individuals for verification

title: Amit Shah inaugurates India’s first immersive language museum in Kolkata
content: The museum features nine galleries exploring different facets of the history of languages and their influence on India’s civilisation and culture; it seeks to present language as a living entity that has continuously shaped society while also being shaped by it

title: B-SMILE scraps ORR redevelopment tender again, to audit potential encroachments along the stretch
content: The development follows directions from the Greater Bengaluru Development Minister, Krishna Byre Gowda, who instructed the Special Purpose Vehicle to hold a public consultation before finalising the redevelopment design

title: Faced with outrage, Kerala Health Minister makes a U-turn on DYFI food packet distribution in hospitals
content: K. Muraleedharan said that the government would put an end to the distribution of food packets on hospital premises under the banner of political parties. His office issues a statement the next day that it need not be perceived as a general statement and pertained to one hospital

title: Telangana government sets up panel to audit private universities as per Supreme Court directions
content: Following Supreme Court directions in the Ayesha Jain case, Telangana has constituted a high-level committee to inspect all private universities and submit a detailed affidavit on governance, finances, admissions and regulatory compliance

title: Police in Madhya Pradesh move activist on hunger strike at Ken-Betwa site to hospital
content: The Congress’s Leader of the Opposition in the Madhya Pradesh Assembly Umang Singhar slams police action, comparing it with that of the Delhi Police against Sonam Wangchuk; ‘the BJP government has made suppressing every dissenting voice its governing model’, he says

title: Fishers seeks withdrawal of nod for large fishing vessels off Kerala coast
content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more .
